name: pip
context: Install, manage, and audit Python packages with pip — use when adding dependencies, creating requirements files, or configuring package sources
info: This skill covers pip, the default package installer for Python. It explains installing packages with `pip install`, managing project dependencies via `requirements.txt` and constraints files, configuring pip through `pip.conf`/`pip.ini` and environment variables, working with private registries, auditing dependencies for security vulnerabilities, and managing the pip cache. pip resolves and downloads packages from PyPI (the Python Package Index) and installs them into the active Python environment. Always use pip inside a virtual environment to avoid polluting the system Python. Sub-topics include version pinning strategies (`==`, `>=`, `~=`), editable installs for local development (`pip install -e .`), hash-checking mode for supply-chain security, and integration with `pip-audit` for vulnerability scanning. See [[python/venv]] for virtual environment setup, [[python/poetry]] and [[python/uv]] for alternative dependency managers.
body:
# pip — Python Package Installer

pip is the default package installer for Python. It downloads and installs packages from the Python Package Index (PyPI) at https://pypi.org and installs them into the active Python environment.

## Installing Packages

```bash
# Install a single package
pip install requests

# Install a specific version
pip install requests==2.31.0

# Install with minimum version
pip install "requests>=2.28.0"

# Install compatible release (same major.minor)
pip install "requests~=2.31.0"

# Install multiple packages
pip install requests flask sqlalchemy

# Install with extras
pip install "fastapi[standard]"
pip install "project[dev,test]"

# Install from requirements file
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Editable install for local development
pip install -e .
pip install -e ".[dev]"

# Upgrade a package
pip install --upgrade requests
pip install -U pip setuptools wheel

# Install to user site-packages
pip install --user requests

# Install to a custom directory
pip install --target ./lib requests

# Uninstall a package
pip uninstall requests
pip uninstall -y requests  # skip confirmation
```

## Managing Dependencies

```bash
# Freeze current environment to requirements.txt
pip freeze > requirements.txt

# Install everything from requirements file
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Use a constraints file to pin transitive deps
pip install -r requirements.txt -c constraints.txt

# List installed packages
pip list
pip list --outdated
pip list --format=json

# Show details about a package
pip show requests

# Check available versions of a package
pip index versions requests

# Check for dependency conflicts
pip check
```

## requirements.txt Format

```text
# Pinned versions (reproducible builds)
requests==2.31.0
flask==3.0.0
sqlalchemy==2.0.23

# Minimum version
requests>=2.28.0

# Compatible release (~= means >=2.31.0, <2.32.0)
requests~=2.31.0

# Version exclusion
requests>=2.28.0,!=2.30.0

# Extras
fastapi[standard]==0.104.1
uvicorn[standard]==0.24.0

# Editable local package
-e .

# Include another requirements file
-r requirements-base.txt

# Apply constraints
-c constraints.txt

# With hashes for supply-chain security
requests==2.31.0 \
    --hash=sha256:abcdef1234567890
```

## pip Configuration

### pip.conf (Linux/macOS) / pip.ini (Windows)

```ini
# Linux/macOS: ~/.config/pip/pip.conf or ~/.pip/pip.conf
# Windows: %APPDATA%\pip\pip.ini
# Per-project: <venv>/pip.conf

[global]
index-url = https://pypi.org/simple
extra-index-url = https://private.registry.example.com/simple/
trusted-host = private.registry.example.com
timeout = 60
no-cache-dir = false
require-virtualenv = true

[install]
no-deps = false
compile = true
```

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `PIP_INDEX_URL` | Override the default PyPI index URL |
| `PIP_EXTRA_INDEX_URL` | Additional package index (space-separated) |
| `PIP_NO_CACHE_DIR` | Disable the pip cache when set to `1` |
| `PIP_REQUIRE_VIRTUALENV` | Refuse to install outside a virtualenv when set to `1` |
| `PIP_TRUSTED_HOST` | Skip TLS verification for a host |
| `PIP_TIMEOUT` | Network timeout in seconds |
| `PIP_CONFIG_FILE` | Path to a custom pip configuration file |

## Private Registries

```bash
# Install from a private index
pip install --index-url https://private.registry.example.com/simple/ my-package

# Use an extra index alongside PyPI
pip install --extra-index-url https://private.registry.example.com/simple/ my-package

# Trust a host without TLS verification
pip install --trusted-host private.registry.example.com --index-url http://private.registry.example.com/simple/ my-package

# Authenticate with a token
pip install --index-url https://__token__:${REGISTRY_TOKEN}@private.registry.example.com/simple/ my-package
```

## Security

```bash
# Install with hash verification
pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.txt

# Generate hashes for requirements
pip hash ./downloads/requests-2.31.0.tar.gz

# Audit installed packages for known vulnerabilities
pip install pip-audit
pip-audit
pip-audit -r requirements.txt
pip-audit --fix
```

## Cache Management

```bash
# Show cache directory
pip cache dir

# List cached packages
pip cache list

# Purge all cached packages
pip cache purge

# Remove a specific package from cache
pip cache remove requests

# Install without using cache
pip install --no-cache-dir requests
```

## Common Commands Reference

```bash
pip install <pkg>                  # Install a package
pip install -r requirements.txt    # Install from file
pip install -e .                   # Editable local install
pip install --upgrade <pkg>        # Upgrade a package
pip install --user <pkg>           # Install to user directory
pip install --target ./lib <pkg>   # Install to custom path
pip uninstall <pkg>                # Remove a package
pip freeze > requirements.txt      # Export installed packages
pip list                           # List installed packages
pip list --outdated                # Show outdated packages
pip show <pkg>                     # Package details
pip index versions <pkg>           # Available versions
pip check                          # Verify dependency compat
pip cache purge                    # Clear the cache
pip-audit                          # Vulnerability scan
```

sections.title: Installing and uninstalling packages
sections.body: Use `pip install <pkg>` to install from PyPI. Pin versions with `==`, set minimums with `>=`, or use compatible release with `~=`. Install extras with `pip install "pkg[extra]"`. Use `-r requirements.txt` to install from a file, `-e .` for editable local development installs, `--upgrade` to update a package, `--user` to install to the user site-packages, and `--target` to install to a custom directory. Remove packages with `pip uninstall <pkg>`. Always install inside a virtual environment.

sections.title: requirements.txt and dependency pinning
sections.body: Use `pip freeze > requirements.txt` to export the current environment. Pin exact versions with `==` for reproducible builds. Use `>=` for minimum versions, `~=` for compatible release constraints, and `!=` to exclude specific versions. Include extras with `pkg[extra]==1.0.0`. Reference other requirement files with `-r base.txt` and apply constraints with `-c constraints.txt`. Constraints files pin transitive dependency versions without declaring them as direct dependencies. Use `--require-hashes` with hash entries for supply-chain integrity.

sections.title: pip.conf, pip.ini, and environment variables
sections.body: Configure pip through `pip.conf` (Linux/macOS at `~/.config/pip/pip.conf`) or `pip.ini` (Windows at `%APPDATA%\pip\pip.ini`). Set the default index with `index-url`, add private registries with `extra-index-url`, and skip TLS checks for internal hosts with `trusted-host`. Key environment variables: `PIP_INDEX_URL` (override default index), `PIP_EXTRA_INDEX_URL` (additional indexes), `PIP_NO_CACHE_DIR` (disable cache), `PIP_REQUIRE_VIRTUALENV` (refuse to install outside a venv). Environment variables override config file values. Command-line flags override both.

sections.title: Security auditing and hash verification
sections.body: Use `--require-hashes` with `pip install -r requirements.txt` to verify package integrity against known hashes — every entry in the requirements file must include a `--hash` line. Generate hashes with `pip hash <file>`. Use `pip-audit` (install via `pip install pip-audit`) to scan installed packages for known vulnerabilities from the OSV database. Run `pip-audit --fix` to auto-upgrade vulnerable packages. Combine with `pip-audit -r requirements.txt` to audit before installing. Never install packages outside a virtual environment in production.

links.context: Create and manage virtual environments for isolating pip installs

related.note: Always install packages inside a virtual environment to avoid polluting the system Python
Poetry provides an all-in-one dependency manager with lockfile support and pyproject.toml-based configuration
uv is a fast Rust-based drop-in replacement for pip with deterministic dependency resolution

scripts.name: Install from requirements.txt
scripts.description: Install Python packages from a requirements.txt file inside the active virtual environment

packages.description: List of package names (with optional version specifiers) to install

requirements_file.description: Path to a requirements.txt file to install from

installed_packages.description: List of packages successfully installed with their resolved versions

depends.reason: Always install packages inside a virtual environment to avoid system-level conflicts
